sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Castle Biosciences CEO Maetzold sells shares worth $106,598
feed.summary: Castle Biosciences Inc (NASDAQ:CSTL) President and CEO Derek J. Maetzold sold 4,400 shares of common stock for $106,598 on March 26, 2026, under a pre-arranged trading plan. Despite the stock's recent decline and being undervalued according to InvestingPro, the company recently surpassed Q4 2025 earnings and revenue expectations. Analysts have maintained a Buy rating, citing strong revenue growth and positive data presented from its DecisionDx-Melanoma test.

feed.title: Castle Biosciences (CSTL) CEO executes 10b5-1 share sales, exercises options
feed.summary: Castle Biosciences (CSTL) President and CEO Derek J. Maetzold recently exercised stock options for 550 shares and subsequently sold 4,172 common shares at a weighted-average price of $25.551, all part of a pre-planned Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. Following these transactions, Maetzold directly holds 21,479 common shares and 13,732 options, in addition to significant indirect holdings through family trusts. This activity was detailed in a recent SEC Form 4 filing, outlining both direct and indirect beneficial ownership changes.

feed.title: Creative Realities (NASDAQ: CREX) delays 10‑K after CDM acquisition, financing
feed.summary: Creative Realities (NASDAQ: CREX) has delayed the filing of its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, notifying the SEC it will utilize the 15-calendar-day extension. The delay is attributed to substantial work stemming from three significant fourth-quarter transactions: the acquisition of the CDM Business, a private placement of Series A Convertible Preferred Stock, and a refinancing of credit facilities. These complex integration efforts and financial transactions consumed internal resources and delayed the completion of the consolidated financial statement audit and internal control assessments.

feed.title: CURX: IPO proceeds fund R&D for Phyto-N; no revenue, $4.2M net loss, and internal control weaknesses
feed.summary: Curanex Pharmaceuticals Inc. (CURX) completed its IPO in 2025, raising $17.25 million, but reported no revenue and a net loss of $4.2 million due to increased R&D and general & administrative expenses. Its flagship product, Phyto-N, is in preclinical development, with an IND submission for ulcerative colitis planned for late 2026. The company faces ongoing material weaknesses in internal controls and risks related to its Nasdaq listing.

feed.title: CSP, Inc. Stock Plummets to New 52-Week Low of $7.55
feed.summary: CSP, Inc., a microcap software and consulting company, has fallen to a new 52-week low of $7.55, reflecting a 44.65% decline over the past year. The company is grappling with profitability issues, stagnant sales growth, and operational inefficiencies, despite a high dividend yield. Its market performance significantly trails the S&P 500, raising investor concerns.

The agreement was signed on March 26, 2026, and no borrowings were outstanding at the time of the extension.

feed.title: Gen Z turns to parents as Americans reshape financial goals, Wells Fargo study finds
feed.summary: A new Wells Fargo study reveals that Americans are redefining financial success, with Gen Z increasingly prioritizing entrepreneurship and relying more on parental support. The study highlights a growing interest in AI for financial guidance and ongoing financial pressures affecting various generations. These shifts indicate a population actively adapting to new economic realities, balancing innovation, evolving goals, and intergenerational support for financial future control.

feed.title: 3M Company Stock: Diversified Industrial Leader Faces Transformation Amid Legal Resolutions and Portfolio Shifts
feed.summary: 3M Company is undergoing a significant transformation, marked by portfolio shifts and legal resolutions, as it refocuses on its core industrial strengths. The company, known for its diversified portfolio across safety, health, and consumer markets, recently spun off its Health Care business into Solventum Corporation. This strategic realignment aims to enhance shareholder value and strengthen 3M's competitive edge through continued innovation and adaptation to market trends, particularly for North American investors.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley upgrades Highwoods Properties stock rating to Equalweight
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has upgraded Highwoods Properties Inc. (NYSE:HIW) to Equalweight from Underweight, while lowering its price target to $23.00. The upgrade reflects the firm's view that the stock's significant multiple contraction has already occurred, and the prior underweight thesis has played out. Key headwinds such as occupancy, dividend risk, and capital recycling are now considered reflected in the share price, with a potential dividend cut seen as a positive catalyst.

feed.title: RTX Awarded $3.81B Contract for F-35 Jet Engines
feed.summary: RTX has been awarded a $3.81 billion contract modification for the production of F135 propulsion systems for the F-35 Joint Strike Fighter program. This contract finalizes Lot 18 engine production and includes additional F135 engines for Lot 19 F-35 aircraft, supporting the U.S. Air Force, Marine Corps, Navy, F-35 cooperative partners, and Foreign Military Sales customers. The F135 engine, developed by Pratt & Whitney, powers all F-35 variants, with an Engine Core Upgrade currently in development for future Block 4 configurations.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Unveils GaN-Based Motor Control Reference Design
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics has introduced EVSTDRVG611MC, a new gallium nitride (GaN)-based reference design board for motor control in domestic appliances and industrial applications. This design leverages GaN's lower switching losses to reduce heat generation and eliminate the need for a heatsink in applications up to 600 watts, leading to more compact and cost-effective modules. The board integrates ST's STDRIVEG611 gate driver and an STM32G431 microcontroller, and is available for $125.06.
feed.source: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media
feed.source_domain: THE ELEC, Korea Electronics Industry Media

feed.title: Qilin Ransomware allegedly breached chemical manufacturer giant Dow Inc
feed.summary: The Qilin ransomware group has claimed a breach of Dow Inc., adding the chemical manufacturing giant to its Tor data leak site. However, no concrete proof of the hack has been released yet. Dow Inc. is a major global chemical company with approximately 36,000 employees and $40 billion in annual revenue, operating in over 160 countries. The Qilin operation, active since 2022, is known for its double-extortion tactics and affiliates recently formed an alliance with DragonForce and LockBit ransomware groups to enhance attack capabilities.

feed.title: Aptiv (APTV) Sees Its Subsidiary Launch Modulus
feed.summary: Aptiv PLC (NYSE: APTV) subsidiary, Winchester Interconnect, has launched Modulus, a new modular connector system designed for next-generation Low Earth Orbit satellite initiatives and unmanned aerial vehicles. The system integrates high-speed single pair Ethernet and modular power, aiming to future-proof high-density platforms. UBS recently upgraded Aptiv to Buy, citing potential value unlocking from the upcoming Versigent spin-off.
